verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of the F1 catalytic core of mitochondrial ATP synthase. The protein is a soluble component of the F1 complex, which resides in the mitochondrial matrix and is not membrane-anchored. While ATP synthase does have a membrane-spanning Fo component, ATP5F1B itself is part of the soluble F1 catalytic core that associates with Fo via non-covalent interactions but does not directly contact the membrane. The protein is strictly mitochondrial-matrix-resident with no documented cell-surface display, PM cycling, lysosomal exocytosis, or cell-state-induced externalization. No therapeutic programs target this protein at the cell surface. Not a candidate for any contextual surface bucket.
